error.regex: (NOAUTH|Authentication required|NOAUTH Authentication|ERR Client sent AUTH.*no password|invalid password|AUTH.*failed)

verdict.summary: Redis requires authentication but the client did not send AUTH, or sent wrong credentials. Redis 7+ supports ACL users.

dead_ends.action: Disable requirepass in redis.conf to remove authentication
dead_ends.why_fails: Removes all access control; any client can read/write/flush all data. Critical security risk.

dead_ends.action: Hardcode the password in application source code
dead_ends.why_fails: Credentials in source code get committed to version control and exposed in logs

workarounds.action: Configure the client library with the correct password
workarounds.how: redis-py: Redis(host='...', password='secret'); or redis-cli -a secret

workarounds.action: Use ACL users for fine-grained access control (Redis 7+)
workarounds.how: ACL SETUSER appuser on >password ~app:* +get +set  # limit commands and key patterns

workarounds.action: Store credentials in environment variables or a secrets manager
workarounds.how: REDIS_URL=redis://:password@host:6379/0; or use HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager
